reviews.review_text: Beautiful setting among the trees, near walking and bike tracks. Camping services cleaned regularly and in great condition. Beautiful birdlife
Absolutely loved this park. Everything was very clean and maintained. Beautiful bush sites. Communal area was excellent. Lot's of walks close by.

Wonderful park, great facilities and beautiful staff
The only information I would pass on is to know the safari tents are extremely close together so do bear this in mind when booking your stay
Beautiful and well maintained campsite with a communal fire pit. Very kids friendly.

Clean campsite with great facilities. The ladies at reception were very helpful and friendly—made us feel welcome right away. Bike rentals are available and highly recommended for exploring the surrounding trails. Would definitely stay again!

Sehr schöner Campingplatz mit sehr freundlichem Personal. Ich habe eine Nacht auf dem Campingplatz mit meinem Camper verbracht. Der Camper ist leider am Abend kaputt gegangen und nicht mehr angesprungen, sodass ich am nächsten Morgen nicht um 10 Uhr auschecken und den Campingplatz verlassen konnte. Alle waren sehr verständnisvoll und herzlich und haben mich aufgrund der Autopanne länger bleiben lassen. Die Toiletten und Duschen sind sehr sauber und die Stellplätze sehr schön in mitten von Bäumen gelegen. Nur zu empfehlen! Danke an das Team für Euer Verständnis und eure Unterstützung! :)

My favorite camping site so far! The forest is amazing for both a walking trail or mountain biking. I highly recommend hiring a mountain bike in the reception. The price is not the cheapest but the bike quality and condition is definitely worth it. The mountain biking trails are diverse and exciting. Apart from the forest trail, the powered sites in the forest is perfect and the amenities are also one of the best. The price of the site is also not pricey
Lovely accommodation and staff members, very well maintained amenities and well equipped kitchen!
Stayed for 5 nights at the safari tent with wonderful common kitchen and bathroom amenities. Location is great near the Margaret River town.
Absolutely beautiful tree stay.

We stayed at this campground for the first time and overall it did not disappoint. The powered sites were great, with good shade, and the amenities were spotless and very well maintained. We stayed as a group of two couples over the Australia Day long weekend, just relaxing and enjoying the facilities. The campground itself is lovely and well set up.
Unfortunately, we had a few negative interactions with the manager that strongly impacted our experience and made us feel uncomfortable and, honestly, discriminated against.
We arrived late on Friday night due to heavy traffic coming from Perth and had to set up our camp so we could sleep (a tent and a rooftop tent). While it was past quiet time, we made every effort to be respectful — no music, no loud noise, just setting up our gear as quickly as possible. The manager approached us in a very passive-aggressive manner to tell us it was past quiet time. We explained we had just arrived and were simply setting up, but she replied abruptly that it was late. We fully understand and respect quiet hours (we camp often and always do), but her tone was unnecessary and unfriendly.
We have no issues respecting other guests and their rest, but unfortunately in this opportunity life got between the rules and we had no option to be a bit noisy past quiet time.

On checkout day, we had another unpleasant interaction. We were sitting in our vehicles, fully packed and ready to leave, waiting for one of our group members who was unwell and using the toilet. The manager approached us and asked if we were staying. I replied that we were checking out, and she responded, “So you should be gone by now.” I explained we were just waiting for our sick friend. We were not using any facilities, just parked and ready to go. I have never been treated like this in any campground or accommodation before.
What made this even more upsetting is that nearby there was a family in a very similar situation who did not appear to be approached or rushed. This made us genuinely feel singled out and judged, possibly because of how we looked or our background.
I strongly recommend that this manager receive proper customer service and cultural awareness training.
To be clear, the campground and infrastructure are excellent, and the reception staff were friendly and helpful. The only downside was this manager (shoulder-length dark hair, glasses), whose attitude turned what should have been a great stay into a disappointing experience.

This was the best campsite we’ve ever stayed at!! Staff so friendly. Location great. Facilities fantastic! Industrial kitchen facilities which were spotlessly clean. Hot showers with decent pressure & bathrooms kept so clean also! 100% would be recommending to others. Thank you!

Terrible powered sites. We couldn’t even charge our camper because the power and water station was so far away. We received an extension cable, but it was not usable. We asked for a fair solution, but they didn’t even try to find one. Customer service (2 women at the office) is incompetent and terrible. I would definitely not recommend this campsite. We also couldn’t rent bikes on the day we left. I would recommend renting bikes in the city, they looked much better anyway.

Loved our stay in the safari tent. 3,4,15 & 16yr old kids had a lovely time. Great kitchen, laundry, bathroom & family bathroom with bath for little kids. Close to main town. Will come again and highly recommend

This stay was extremely disappointing, especially for the price paid. The cabin was not clean on arrival bins were full, blankets were dirty, and flyscreens had holes. There was no internet for the entire stay. Basic items requested (towels and dishwashing liquid) were only delivered the next day.Reception staff were rude and dismissive when issues were raised. Overall, the accommodation and service fell well below an acceptable standard. I would not recommend staying here.

Absolutely amazing 4 days we stayed in the safari tent and it was perfect beds were so comfortable and everything needed was available, kitchen facilities were spot on for cooking urself and a short walk through wallys trail brought you out to the start of the town. 10/10
Excellent

Unclean accommodation with dirty bedding, overflowing rubbish bins, no internet throughout the entire stay, delayed response to basic requests, and a rude, dismissive receptionist. Overall a very poor experience and not the standard expected from RAC with the amount that I paid for the Cabin.Would not recommend.
Lovely location. Caretaker is very rude though. The old woman there would benefit from some training in manners.
Great for families! Daily kids activity.

Amazing location, super close to margs
Very nice staff and super clean facilities

So so good for camping or glamping or for booking a cabin for the family! Extremely incredible kitchen with huge ovens and lots of bench space for food prep. There are bike trails galore and it is really geared towards a family getaway or couples who want to stay somewhere nestled in the forest of Margaret River or want to just enjoy the cycling trails.

We stayed here for one night and had issues with leaking water in our van. The staff member who was working that day (wasn’t sure about his name, looked like a Dylan from the UK) was so friendly and super helpful even though that was beyond his work tasks. 10/10 can recommend! Lovely campground, super clean, and our favorite toilet facilities that we have seen on the southwest coast!
Great campsite, clean amenities.
Everything here was just really good

Nice camping zone and vibes. Not too far from a lovely village with shops and restaurants, coffee etc.. very touristic as well during weekend with all campers all around visiting the South. The living zones are well kept and clean in the camping zone and each other respect well every details and noise for specific time etc. The little shop can help a lot. Many facilities for campers. Nice atmosphere 👌

This campsite has some of the cleanest toilet and shower facilities of any site I've visited. Very friendly reception staff too.
> Half hour's walk from the main town (2 mins by car).
> Lovely forest setting. Easy access to biking/hiking trails.
> Large communal area with a firepit and camp kitchen.

I loved this place! The sites are large, covered with trees, and have plenty of space between each one, so you feel privacy, quiet, and calm. The amenities were excellent — tidy, clean, and modern. The reception staff were kind and informative. It's a blend of nature and high-quality services and amenities.

The lady who checked us, absolutely 10/10 knew her stuff, a credit to the caravan park 👍👍👍

Beautiful bush surrounds, shower block clean and modern, check it out guys, gets a 👍👍👍👍👍 thumbs up from our family
Great first experience for a city dweller! Thankful for heated blankets. Welcome the birds chirping as dawn breaks.

Powered site review: Loved how bustling it was at night - youth friendly! It's like a private refuge deep in the forest, clean toilets, and just felt so comfortable and safe. Plus the communal fireplace was a lifesaver in Oct cold!
This is an absolute 5 star place! The hot showers, great kitchen and we absolutely loved the fire place in the evening. We stayed 3 nights at a powered campsite and cant wait to be back here!

Very nice campsite with modern and very clean sanitary facilities. There are also several BBQ stations and a communal kitchen, which were also very clean. There is a large open fireplace for everyone to use and there are lot of hiking trails and bike trails around the campsite.
We really enjoyed camping at this caravan park. Beautiful location in the forest. The sites are roomy and most are surrounded by trees and shrubs. Very close to town.
Took the caravan and stayed 3 nights. Beautiful park and close to all the sights in the area. Price was as good as the government bush camps.

I stayed in one of the 2 bedroom cabins for 4 nights, completely self-contained, with a full kitchen including complimentary salt, pepper and instant coffee. Kitchen includes all utensils, and cooking supplies. Bathroom was very nice and clean, complete with towels. Comfortable beds and couch, with all bedding needed. Fire-pit complete with wood and starters.
Reception has bikes for hire, did not utilize this so cannot comment.
All in all a wonderful week away 😊
Were in a caravan.  Park location centrally located for exploring the region. Amenities are excellent.

Great place to stay as a couple or family - have stayed in the Safari Tents and Cabins and enjoy both. Great location and awesome for those that enjoy mountain biking with trails coming off the nature park. The smaller cabins are great during the cooler months, and very cosy with the wood fire going. Been here every year for the past few years and never disappoints. Office staff have always been a pleasure to deal with too ✨

Perfect hideaway in the forest. An old mill lots of walks. Massive camp fire and kitchen plus huge undercover area. 2 lots of amenities with ample toilets and showers. And the lady at the desk was wonderful and helpful. You can even walk through the forest from the park into town!

Beautiful facilities, clean, tidy & the perfect back to nature staycation! Stayed in a glamping Safari tent for a winter break with family joining us. Fantastic walking trails and great facilities for young and old.

From the moment the beautiful staff welcomed us, we felt at home. After months on the road, this peaceful park was exactly what we needed — clean, cozy, and cared for in every way. The modern kitchen, communal fire pit, and bathrooms were beyond amazing, but it was the warm, genuine staff who made our stay unforgettable. A truly special place.

Our stay at the RAC Margaret River Campground was exceptional! The staff were incredibly generous, always ready to assist with any little requests we had. Every day, they lit a communal fire at 4 pm, creating a cozy and welcoming atmosphere for everyone. The warm, new showers were a delight, and the camp kitchen was simply incredible, equipped with everything you could possibly need. Honestly, there's nothing to fault at this campground. The staff truly made our stay memorable with their outstanding service and friendly demeanor. I highly recommend!

Great spot close to town with huge camp kitchen and community undercover fire pit.
Also allowed own campfire 👍

Wonderful campsite. We stayed in the Safari Tent, heater provided and electric blanket on the double. Huge camp kitchen, BBQ, big community fire pit. Great bike tracks for the kids through the bush. Walk tracks to the old chimney and bridge crossing easy even the 3yr old had a great time on the walk.

We had a fantastic stay at this holiday park! The facilities were clean and well-maintained, the staff were incredibly friendly and helpful, and there were plenty of fun activities to keep the kids entertained. It’s a great spot for families, and we’ll definitely be back. Highly recommend!

Beautiful place,set on the outskirts of Margaret  River.
Stayed a night in out van.
Lovely area,very nice amenities.
Great camp area,with an awesome camp fire.
Love being in the national park with the bush all around. Lots of room in the park and good facilities.

I booked 3 nights powered camp site but had to cancel on Sunday which was 4 days ahead of the Thursday arrival date. Did not get any refund for what I had paid (100%) because I was informed there is no refund on cancellation.
A misrepresentation of the conditions on their website.

The room was large and clean with the bathroom and kitchenette separating.  Great service at reception and honestly rare to see.  The room could do with a refresh even though it was clean and well maintained.
Beautiful, our favourite park on our 3 month road trip in WA
Very nice cozy place to stay in. Reception was always nice and friendly.
We will be back !

Great nature park only 2km from town. Big sites right in the middle of nature with power & water. Amenities were brand new and enough to cope with a crowd. The camp kitchen was huge never seen anything like it! You can have a fire and fire pits are available to hire and wood to purchase. |I would definitely stay here rather than the other parks on offer in Margaret River.
Lovely peaceful place to relax

I camped here with a large family group. Mix of tents, swags, camper vans, and caravans. This place is absolute perfection. Gorgeous sites right in the middle of nature, and right next to lots of forest walks. Easy walk through a beautiful forest trailer to Giniversity, and then to town. ||The amenities are great - lovely large shower blocks, cleaned regularly and well maintained. |The sites are all on dirt/gravel so just make sure you’re prepared for a bit of mud and rocks if you’re in a tent. Also make sure you check the fire restrictions if you want to cook on a campfire - currently fires only allowed 5pm-10pm.||This is the most beautiful campsite I’ve been in. Would love to come back.
Nice spot, quiet and peaceful compared to most family camping grounds.
Fabulous bush campground. Very generous pitches. Good location, however the camp kitchen is very small and not very clean.

Cet endroit est une pépite.. en mode caravan park nous avons tout apprécié a cet endroit au coeur de la fôret de Margaret river. Les espaces sont propres, spacieux, fonctionnels et authentique...on a adoré. C'est un endroit magique avec en prime le chant des kookaburas le matin au réveil ... Merveilleux et fantastique endroit.
Top caravan park!
We spent the last week at this amazing holiday destination — the bike trails were so much fun, and the staff were incredibly friendly and welcoming.
Stayed at the lodge, which was a great spacious place, with an amazing front area!
Nice bush camp

Such a lovely experience here, I couldn't fault it! The camp kitchen is more than generous with an oven, 2 burners, and other amenities. The trees surrounding this park are gorgeous and it makes you feel like you're really camping somewhere out bush. I hope we make it back here one day, the town is beautiful.

Abbiamo prenotato una tenda safari.
La tenda non è male, peccato che tra la porta e il pavimento c'è abbastanza spazio da far passare qualsiasi insetto ed eventuali serpenti.
Il posto è inondato da formiche e farfalline.
La cucina non dispone di nessun utensile. Spugne e panni per lavare barbecue praticamente inesistente, vecchi e logori.
Disposizione dei bagni pubblici alquanto discutibile, molto vicini alla cucina ma lontani dalle tende. Andare in bagno di notte non è affatto piacevole, soprattutto quando piove.
Posizione buona ma è l'unico lato positivo.

Great location,  right on the top of the mountain bike tracks. Great tracks for both kids and experienced adults. Facilities are clean and great showers.  But...

First time I stayed it was full, yet still quiet and peaceful. Beautiful peppermint trees and tucked in amongst bushland.

This weekend...yuck. SO noisy. The dorms must have been full of a school group or something and they stayed up past 11pm talking in the mess hall. The noise was amplified incredibly.  At around 730pm, each night about 6 cars came in as a very loud convoy to use the facilities.  I'm sure they were not staying here. Youths on the playground at all hours, right next to people's tents.

In short, fantastic location,  great facilities,  lovely helpful staff, but it's a lucky dip for noise if there's a big group using the mess hall.
Thoroughly enjoyed the experience and location , BUT the shower pressure is WORST I've ever had displeasure of try to wash under

Great location
We enjoyed being slightly out of margs but close enough to be in town in 5 for food.
We booked the safari tent, which was very nice we loved it.
We definitely will be back

The atmosphere is tranquil, fresh relaxed. Literally in amongst the trees. Friendly wildlife. Just be prepared it is dusty when dry and I imagine muddy when wet. Room finishes could you some TLC especially if you want to keep the mosquitoes out

My partner and I are currently moving a lot on the road in a van and we were DESPERATE for a hot shower. Everywhere was booked including this park but they really went above and beyond in helping us out and offering a place to stay for the night. We are beyond grateful. The nicest staff I’ve ever met at a caravan park. Thank you 🙏🏼

Lodges are a hit or miss some amenities are great like the sofa, dinner table, barbeque and general furniture.  However things like the toilet door lock are broken, the drawer in the bathroom has a large hole in it, the toilet door constantly gets stuck and some rooms are quite dusty with sheets that are quite stained.

Not much service or replacements for towels so dry them whenever you get the chance.

The location in your bush is a high point not much for kids to do so plan out daily trips.

Der Platz ist mitten im Wald und bietet entsprechend viel Schatten. Es ist sehr gemütlich. Die Waschküche hat relativ neue Geräte, alles ist sehr sauber. |Der Platz ist etwas ausserhalb des Dorfes, mobil sollte man sein. |Wir kommen gerne wieder.

Loved the amazing experience of this spot. The kids loved it, and the amenities are clean alongside the staff being very friendly. The tents are tidy, the beds so comfortable that include the bunk beds and they provide a lot of privacy too.
Beautiful place, right next to the mountain bike trails.
Excellent facilities, clean toilets and showers, spacious sites and super friendly staff. Sue and Graig were wonderful!
We stayed in a van, self accommodated and it was fabulous. Friendly and helpful, the staff enable your stay to be hassle free. Clean facilities and plenty of space.

I go to the RAC Nature Park for 2 weeks every year with my family (sometimes more). It is always a relief when you turn up and you know you get to relax and go mountain biking. The facilities in the park are awesome. Will be coming for many years to come.

Stayed here 7 nights to explore the Margaret River area.  Excellent park for our purposes.  A couple of km out town which is ideal for a walk or a short bike ride.  Turn down Carters Rd and a 500 mt gravel track leads you to the entrance to the park.  This park provides all your caravanning needs without all the bells and whistles and accompanying pricing.  The sites are large with plenty of space between sites, flat level and with easy access and space for reversing.  Abulution blocks are clean modern and regular cleaned with good water pressure.  Rare park that you can have a camp fire to 10pm outside of fire restriction period.  Organise a firepit and wood from the office.  Staff were friendly.  This being an eco park there are lots of trees with those using solar only will have difficulty chasing the sun.  2 other c/parks in M/River are tetris/sardine like being the reason we liked it out here.  There is a very chilled relaxed vibe.  Easy walk into town.  Bike track into town as well as well as mountain bike track area close by.  Follow Carters Rd right out to Caves Rd where most wineries are located on.  While at M/River defintely visit Lake Cave (instgramable) & Jewel Cave.  The two best 'local' caves.  Lots of bird life at this c/park and can highly recommend this park as first choice for your  M/River visit.  Travel well and stay safe.

Absolutely beautiful place. Apart from the idiots speeding in and out of the single lane access, this would have to be one of the nicest caravan parks we've ever stayed at. Surprising considering some of the other RAC parks we've seen.
Great location and very clean

Great caravan sites and very friendly and welcoming staff.
Fire wood when the season allows is spot on - don’t bother take a run into town, just ask the team at reception.
We always enjoy our stays here.

It was a terrible experience. I booked bulk bed rooms for my family, but it was super noisy. A few people did not follow the quiet policy between 10:00 pm to 8:00 am, but kept talking loudly in the dorm. It was difficult to get to sleep in such a noisy environment. Called the emergency call of the office, someone answered the phone and promised to come and have a look, and then no one came to help us. I called it many times later and no one answered the my call any more. Felt very frustrated. I strongly don’t recommend to book the bulk bed rooms.
Not the greatest service, very rude employees, would suggest better places to stay in Margaret river

Beautiful cabin with all the amenities. Close to the centre of town and surrounds. Staff were excellent, anything that needed fixing was sorted out straight away. Only thing I can fault was the lighting in the cabin kitchen and living area could be a lot brighter. It was difficult to prepare food in the kitchen and also to play board games at the table as the lighting was very dim and disproportional.
Such a beautiful spot - walk through the Forrest to town!

Loved it here|Clean enough bathrooms. Sagging and dripping shower heads but that doesn’t affect my stay - only RAC water usage. Had to keep turning the taps off after other users left them dripping for hours. |Sue in the office is very helpful. |Our site backed onto a large green space which was handy for play. Lovely birds and possums, huge kitchen and outdoor undercover area, washing machines etc. 5/5

We absolutely love this park, one of our favourite places to stay .Always have a great time. Very peaceful, we love that can have a fire every afternoon/night bathrooms always clean and maintained as well as kitchen areas Staff friendly and helpful

I'm disappointed that it is a rac resort with a few things. Paper towels were not filled for 2 days in the main kitchen. The bike ride prices were quoted $30 for one day and then become $50 the next day. Don't know how.
The bike and walking tracks are good.
Extremely good location. Inspite of our experience we will come back as they are minor compared to overall experience.
Love  staying here manager's are great friendly staff very helpful 👍 peaceful and relaxing very clean caravan Park.
Amazing
Great location for camping close to town with fire pits.

We stayed on a powered site for 4 nights. Loved the serenity and nature all around. Quite large site which was great. The amenities are newish I’d say and kept extremely clean. Well done. Loved exploring the bike and running trails in behind the park. Good run or ride into Margaret River town from here

Great campsite, really accomodating staff. Really good, large common area and kitchen which was clean. Same goes for showers and toilets. Only 5 minute drive from Margaret River town and nestled in the peaceful forest.

Usually avoid caravan parks whenever possible (preferring remote or national park camps), but this one served a purpose being close to wineries and in thick forest. Quiet (vague hum from the highway not noticeable most of the time), and camp sites not on top of each other like many caravan parks.

Staff here were helpful and calm when on arrival our car broke down in the driveway blocking access for new arrivals. Can't thank them enough for their easy demeanour and helpful suggestions. The camp kitchen is well equipped and the undercover eating/entertainment area is huge. Amenities were always clean and plenty of facilities available. Can't thank the staff enough, and the coffee on site was a bonus.

Stayed in one of the new Safari tents at the park. Great accommodation for 4-6 people (adults and kids) with the double bed and 2 bunks. Facilities well maintained for the kitchen and ablution blocks.
Very close to Margaret River town, walkable if you don't mind walking a couple of clicks through the bush track.

All round fantastic. Everything that makes for a memerable camping, glamping, and accommodation experience for all the right reason. There is something for everyone from the cycling trails to food and entertainment and all within walking distance (1.5 km). Handy reasonable convenience store. A fully functioning kitchen available.

Google is only allowing me to give 4 stars, because I can’t comment on the rooms & service requirements for the survey. We’re here in a camper trailer. It’s so nice to have a campground with full facilities; but in the bush with large campsites. Fantastic hikes & mountain bike trails well marked out. Fun exploring activities for kids in school holidays.
There’s no pool, but you really don’t need it with so much else to do in the area.
If you’re a little less mobile, I’d suggest asking for a campground closer to the new ablutions block. My only negative is that paths to facilities aren’t easy to navigate or well lit.
Great for the price, good cell signal. 80% comforts of home, 20% roughing it.
Great place to stay for the whole family or on your own

Little quite slice of paradise - hidden in the trees/bushland away from the road. very nice set up - we had a powered site and it was clean and the amenities were spotless  (ones near the office) camp kitchen was huge and its a very kid friendly place with a playground.
Beautiful location, very helpful staff
Loved it.

Situated just outside of Margaret River, this campground is nestled amidst the natural beauty of the bushland. It offers spacious and comfortable campsites, along with excellent amenities. The friendly and welcoming staff are always ready to assist guests. Please note that all roads within the park, including the main access, are unpaved and may need to be considered during rainy weather.
Toller Platz im Wald mit viel schatten und 5 min mit dem Auto in die Stadt. Sehr grosse parzellen mit viel Privatsphäre, ein Gebäude mit Sanitären Anlagen ist sehr neu.

What a great place to stay. Van spots are large and we'll seperated. The staff are helpful and super friendly. Craig helped me out deluxe with something I needed to get done. Perfect location and central to everything.

Had a caravan site here so can’t comment on rooms. The location is beautiful snug amongst the trees. Very generous site sizes. Great back to nature feel. Huge camp kitchen. I would absolutely stay here again. I usually don’t pay more than $40 a site but would pay the $50 to stay again. It’s lovely here.

We really loves the RAC campground, the people working were absolutely super stars and so helpful. My one complaint is that in the bathroom and kitchen near the mill (where we stayed), it seems like every single water tap is leaking. And when I say leaking, I mean some of them are just dumping out water and you cannot close them off. This was a bit upsetting to us as WA is already having water troubles. Lovely place to stay but, RAC PLEASE FIX YOUR TAPS!!  You're a large company and would be saving so much water from being wasted. Thanks again to staff! Beautiful stay :)(

Overall=
A good bush type experience with resources families need:
Bbqs, laundry, playground and free decent wifi for when you need it.

Stayed in the biggest dorm.
Sleeps 26. Dorm has private door to a split level room with 13 singke bunks and 2 single beds there's 2 steps to half the beds. The fire escape is off the ground level.
There are a few windows and some pedestal fans as there's no air conditioning.
A (shared) communal living/ dining area with 3 other dorms. A huge outside undercover area . Small semi commercial communal kitchen.  3 fridges, a microwave, and a couple of toasters.

The negs=
There is no soundproofing between dorms and the mattresses were dusty.
No pool

The pos=
A real bush experience: dust and all 😄
Close to everything
Reasonable price to sleep many!
Amenities for families: laundry, disabled access, cooking facilities, Bbqs

Note: Access road not graded but accessible by a regular car (not low peofile tyres).

Disabled access via smooth paths in-between communal buildings but bumpy dirt roads otherwise. Keys required for disabled bathroom access.

This is a good park for caravans, campers and it does have a couple of cabins. The camp kitchen is fantastic and the facilities clean. It isn’t as close to Margaret river as we thought as we wanted to walk in and out.  At this time of year it’s pretty dusty so we didn’t walk the dirt roads. I didn’t like the road in to the park as I was worried about what could happen when caravans are coming and going at the same time.

Less than a five minute drive (20 minute walk) from Margaret River town, the RAC Margaret River Nature Park is a great place to stay. Great for being out of the action yet close to town; it puts you in good stead to explore the region. The facilities are excellent, and the safari tent is luxurious.
Thank you to Nicole and the team!

Sarah and isn have been such great hosts.
Have loved being Herr thankyou just for making it such an enjoyable stay.
Debbie and ian

We had a wonderful time drifting around all the Margaret River region has to offer and RAC was perfectly located for that.  It had all the facilities we needed and was great to come 'home' to.  We used the outdoor BBQ everyday and it was roomy enough for.everyone to spread out, indoors on the couch in front of TV or outdoors on the comfy couch under the covered deck.  We especially liked being able to cook breakfast and dinner, which saved us a lot of money, and the supermarkets were so handy.

Beautiful, quiet relaxing location with walking trail and town is within walking distance.

Cabin was immaculate and cosy!

Staff are excellent! Would definitely recommend!
Very dusty
We've been coming here for years and absolutely love the Nature Park. Beautiful location in the forest next to the trails. Walking distance to town. Very helpful and friendly staff. Highly recommend.
Beautiful location, beautiful safari tents! The shared bathrooms are basic but absolutely adequate. Would definitely stay here again!
Gorgeous forest campground. Clean dirt sites are large and amenities are brillant. Close to town. Highly recommend 👌

Perfect location for the mtb trails, walks and short walk into the town, lovely bush setting.
There were very noisy school groups on one of the nights, resulting in very dirty facilities, but was super quiet and clean the next night when they left. Recommend ringing ahead if they have school groups, if you want a relaxing and quiet time here.

walk to town, about 25 mins
absolutely nice, new facilities!
off-season, powered site, rooftoptent, unlimited wifi, rac discount: 37$ very fair
nice kitchen and bbq area, big and roofed
i heard there are movienights
20$ for renting a mountainbike, there are awesome trails!

We've stayed here many times. The modern safari tents are great. Good for a family. We've also had our swag here on a powered site. Great value. Great amenities. Great staff. They even have great coffee 😁

Great shaded Park. Good facilities. Only two things wrong are only 1 clothesline for the whole park. Second is the long narrow driveway . Very awkward when cars meet let alone vans meet and very few areas to move over.
